assertions.text: Output contains at least 2 separate markdown tables
assertions.evidence: The response contains 'Table 1: Sign-Up — Plan & First Charge' and 'Table 2: Cancellation Refund', both rendered as separate markdown tables.

assertions.text: A table or note explicitly captures that the free trial is not available on annual plans (not just implied)
assertions.evidence: Table 1 rows 3 and 4 show 'Trial? = no' for Annual plan subscribers: '| New annual subscriber | Annual | no | no | £95.88 immediately |' and '| New annual subscriber + loyalty | Annual | yes | no | £86.29 immediately |'

assertions.text: At least one table row demonstrates the loyalty programme discount scenario for annual plan subscribers
assertions.evidence: Table 1 row 4: '| New annual subscriber + loyalty | Annual | yes | no | £86.29 immediately |'

assertions.text: The response identifies at least one genuinely underspecified interaction — such as: what happens when a trial subscriber upgrades to annual mid-trial, when a subscriber joins the loyalty programme mid-billing-cycle, or whether a cancellation refund is based on the discounted or full price. These are NOT stated in the rules and require a product decision.
assertions.evidence: Open Questions section lists: '**1. Refund basis for annual loyalty cancellation**... is the prorated refund calculated from: the £86.29 they actually paid, or the £95.88 full annual price?'; '**2. Trial → annual upgrade** Can a subscriber in their 30-day trial switch to an annual plan?'; '**3. Loyalty mid-cycle** An annual subscriber joins the loyalty programme partway through their year.'

assertions.text: The annual/loyalty/trial interaction is explicitly surfaced as an open question — via '?', 'TBD', blank expected value, or a written open question — rather than silently resolved
assertions.evidence: Table 2 row 6 shows '| Cancel mid-year, annual + loyalty | Annual | yes | 180 days in, 185 days remaining | ? |' and Open Question 2 explicitly asks about trial → annual upgrade interactions.

assertions.text: Makes clear (via table structure, a note, or explicit rows) that the loyalty discount applies to the annual plan only — a loyalty member on monthly pays the same as anyone else.
assertions.evidence: Table 1 row 2: '| Loyalty member on monthly | Monthly | yes | yes (30 days) | £9.99 after trial |' and the note: '**Confirm**: The loyalty discount applies only to annual plan subscribers. Row 2 — a loyalty member on monthly pays full price (£9.99/month). Is that correct?'

assertions.text: Surfaces whether a loyalty annual subscriber's prorated cancellation refund is calculated from the discounted price (£86.29) or the list price (£95.88) — as an open question, '?', or note. Not silently resolved.
assertions.evidence: Open Question 1: 'The subscriber paid £86.29. When they cancel mid-year, is the prorated refund calculated from: the £86.29 they actually paid, or the £95.88 full annual price? These give different amounts (~£43.76 vs ~£48.56 for 185 days remaining).'

assertions.text: Includes a cancellation/refund table or section (not just pricing) — the loyalty discount creates a refund calculation ambiguity that should be shown with concrete rows.
assertions.evidence: Table 2 titled 'Cancellation Refund' includes concrete rows: '| Cancel mid-year, annual | Annual | no | 180 days in, 185 days remaining | ~£48.56 (185/365 × £95.88) |' and '| Cancel mid-year, annual + loyalty | Annual | yes | 180 days in, 185 days remaining | ? |'

assertions.text: The cancellation/refund table includes a loyalty member column (or equivalent) to distinguish loyalty vs non-loyalty annual refund rows — not just plan type alone.
assertions.evidence: Table 2 has a 'Loyalty Member?' column with distinct rows: '| Cancel mid-year, annual | Annual | no | ...' vs '| Cancel mid-year, annual + loyalty | Annual | yes | ...' explicitly distinguishing loyalty and non-loyalty annual subscribers.

assertions.text: The '?' suffix is only used on output/expected columns (e.g. 'Refund?', 'Price?') — not on input columns like plan type, loyalty status, or yes/no flags. Input columns should not have '?' suffixes.
assertions.evidence: Table 1 header: '| Scenario | Plan | Loyalty Member? | Trial? | First Charge |' — both 'Loyalty Member?' and 'Trial?' are input columns (loyalty status and yes/no flags) that carry '?' suffixes, violating the assertion. Table 2 header also uses 'Loyalty Member?' as an input column with a '?' suffix.
